differential_diagnoses: Head and neck malignancy (e.g., Squamous cell carcinoma, Lymphoma)
Deep neck space abscess with atypical organism
next_steps: Perform contrast-enhanced CT angiography (CTA) of the neck to rule out pseudoaneurysm
Perform excisional or incisional biopsy of the lesion for histopathology and flow cytometry
Obtain tissue for aerobic, anaerobic, fungal, and acid-fast bacilli cultures
Order laboratory workup: ANCA, IgG4 levels, ESR/CRP, and CBC with differential
Consult interventional radiology for potential endovascular evaluation if vascular anomaly is suspected
Discontinue empirical antibiotics and steroids until definitive tissue diagnosis is obtained

differential_diagnoses: Head and neck malignancy (e.g., Squamous cell carcinoma, Lymphoma)
next_steps: Perform immediate contrast-enhanced CT angiography (CTA) of the neck to characterize the mass and rule out pseudoaneurysm
Consult Interventional Radiology for urgent evaluation and potential endovascular management
Perform flexible fiberoptic nasolaryngoscopy to assess the pharyngeal airway and mass location
Strict avoidance of further biopsy or needle aspiration until vascular etiology is excluded
Obtain tissue for aerobic, anaerobic, fungal, and mycobacterial cultures if surgery/biopsy becomes safe
Order laboratory workup: CBC, ESR, CRP, ANCA, IgG4 levels, and HIV screening
Obtain QuantiFERON-TB Gold or T-spot testing
